executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated to a MAJOR Level 1 (Score 2817) with 465 incidents in the last 24 hours. A coordinated US-Israeli airstrike on Iran on 28 February killed Supreme Leader Khamenei and struck over 20 Iranian provinces. Iran responded with ballistic missile barrages on Israel and strikes on 14+ US bases across Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Qatar, and the Gulf region. IRGC naval forces attacked US support ships. Civilian casualties reported from Iranian school strikes. Regional spillover evident with attacks on Dubai, Oman, and Cyprus. The conflict trajectory indicates rapid escalation with no de-escalation signals.

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is on a rapid escalation trajectory with no evident de-escalation pathways. Iran's leadership vacuum following Khamenei's death creates uncertainty, but IRGC has demonstrated continued commitment to hostilities. US-Israel coalition has shown willingness to conduct sustained operations across 20+ Iranian provinces. Expect continued Iranian retaliatory strikes against US bases and regional allies (Gulf states, Israel). Civilian casualties and attacks on civilian infrastructure (schools) will likely fuel further regional anger and potential popular mobilization. Proxy forces (Hezbollah, Houthis) are mobilizing, expanding the conflict geography.
escalation_risks: Iran uses Fatah-2 hypersonic missiles against US forces - first deployment reported
Hezbollah direct military involvement against Israel could open northern front
Regional states (Saudi Arabia, UAE) increasingly targeted - risk of broader Arab state involvement
Quds Force commander disappearance may trigger erratic proxy responses
Russian-Chinese diplomatic alignment could escalate to direct great power involvement
Nuclear facility attacks could transform conflict category entirely
Attacks on Gulf oil infrastructure could trigger global energy crisis
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No immediate de-escalation pathways visible. Iraq has called for sovereignty respect and return to diplomacy but lacks leverage. UN Security Council session highlighted deep divisions - no consensus on conflict resolution. Iran's leadership vacuum may either trigger internal collapse or consolidate IRGC hardline control with intensified military posture. GCC emergency diplomatic efforts ongoing but unlikely to influence current escalation momentum without major power intervention.
